q: What is the key physiological difference between Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes?
options: Type 1 results from insulin resistance in target cells; Type 2 from destruction of alpha cells
Type 1 is caused by autoimmune destruction of beta cells, eliminating insulin production; Type 2 involves insulin resistance in target cells with insufficient compensatory insulin
Type 1 only occurs in adults; Type 2 is exclusively a childhood disease
Both types involve excess glucagon but differ only in the degree of hyperglycemia
explanation: In Type 1, the immune system destroys beta cells so no insulin is made; in Type 2, cells become resistant to insulin's signal, and beta cells may eventually fail to compensate.
